occupations.title: Economists
occupations.education: Master's degree typically required; some government positions accept bachelor's; PhD needed for senior roles
occupations.description: Economists conduct research, prepare reports, and formulate plans to address economic problems. They study the production and distribution of goods and services, analyze data, and advise businesses and governments on economic policy.
topIndustries: Management, scientific, and technical consulting services
Research and development in the social sciences
keyDuties: Research and analyze economic issues
Conduct surveys and collect data
Analyze data using mathematical models and statistical techniques
Prepare reports, tables, and charts
Interpret and forecast market trends
Advise businesses and governments on economic decisions

occupations.title: Financial Analysts
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree required; MBA or CFA often preferred for advancement
occupations.description: Financial analysts provide guidance to businesses and individuals making investment decisions. They assess the performance of stocks, bonds, and other investments and help companies make capital allocation decisions.
topIndustries: Securities, commodity contracts, and financial investments
keyDuties: Analyze financial statements and data
Study economic and business trends
Evaluate current and historical financial data
Prepare written reports and recommendations
Meet with company officials to gain better insight
Recommend individual investments and investment portfolios

occupations.title: Data Scientists
occupations.growthDescription: Much faster than average - 4th fastest growing occupation
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree in computer science, mathematics, statistics, or related field; master's degree often preferred
occupations.description: Data scientists use analytical tools and techniques to extract meaningful insights from data. They develop algorithms, build models, and apply machine learning to solve complex business problems.
topIndustries: Computer systems design and related services
keyDuties: Identify valuable data sources and automate collection processes
Clean and validate data to ensure accuracy
Analyze large datasets to find trends and patterns
Build predictive models and machine learning algorithms
Present findings to stakeholders using visualizations
Develop AI and machine learning solutions
growthFactors: Growing demand for AI model development
Increased need for data-driven business decisions
Integration of data applications into business practices

occupations.title: Management Analysts (Consultants)
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree required; MBA often preferred; specific certifications like CMC valuable
occupations.description: Management analysts, often called management consultants, recommend ways to improve an organization's efficiency. They advise managers on how to make organizations more profitable through reduced costs and increased revenues.
topIndustries: Management, scientific, and technical consulting services
Computer systems design and related services
keyDuties: Gather and organize information about problems to be solved
Interview personnel and conduct on-site observations
Analyze financial and operational data
Develop solutions and recommend new systems or changes
Present findings and recommendations to management
Help implement recommended changes
occupations.workEnvironment: Most work as consultants on a contractual basis. Frequent travel to client sites required.

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree; master's degree may be required for senior positions
occupations.description: Market research analysts study market conditions to examine potential sales of a product or service. They help companies understand what products people want, who will buy them, and at what price.
topIndustries: Management, scientific, and technical consulting services
Computer systems design and related services

occupations.title: Investment Banking Analysts
occupations.jobGrowthNote: Competitive field; varies by market conditions and deal activity
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree required; top programs and networking essential
occupations.description: Investment banking analysts help companies raise capital, execute mergers and acquisitions, and provide strategic advisory services. This is an intensive, high-compensation entry point into finance.
keyDuties: Build financial models (DCF, LBO, merger models)
Create pitch books and client presentations
Conduct due diligence and company research
Support deal execution and documentation
Analyze comparable companies and precedent transactions
occupations.careerPath: Analyst (2-3 years) → Associate (3 years) → VP (3 years) → Director/ED → Managing Director

occupations.title: Economic Consultants
occupations.jobGrowthNote: Strong demand in litigation support, antitrust, and regulatory matters
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree minimum; Master's or PhD often required for senior roles
occupations.description: Economic consultants apply economic theory and quantitative methods to analyze business situations, often for litigation support, antitrust analysis, or regulatory proceedings.
keyDuties: Analyze economic data for legal cases
Conduct econometric analysis
Prepare expert reports and testimony support
Research industry and market dynamics
Quantify damages in litigation

occupations.title: Policy Analysts
occupations.education: Bachelor's degree; Master's in Public Policy (MPP) or related field preferred
occupations.description: Policy analysts research, analyze, and evaluate economic, social, and political issues to provide advice to policymakers, government agencies, and organizations.

generalOutlook.summary: Economics graduates have diverse career options spanning finance, consulting, technology, policy, and academia. The quantitative and analytical skills developed in an economics program are highly valued across industries.
